name: The White House
subject: President Trump Delivers First Speech to United Nations
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=97acad780185642f328513dc376cb406b41733bfbb152aad72e6f19e1f90a55de1f92006d2d2ef5617704f367f19c55439d2890f93b7eece

Yesterday, President Donald J. Trump spoke for the first time at the United Nations General Assembly (UNGA) on the need for international cooperation and UN reform. The President attended bilateral meetings with Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel and President Emmanuel Macron of France. President Trump and Prime Minister Netanyahu affirmed the historic bond and continued cooperation between the United States and Israel. President Trump will address the General Assembly this morning.
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=97acad780185642f206ce4a6dfc58a2c934a541430dd0ba65437d0ee5f226f9155e64b22e3b4eae598a975235761f9b59fe916e2844b7ac4
Watch the speech.

-------------

United Nations General Assembly

President Trump is participating in the 72nd United Nations General Assembly this week. The President is meeting with world leaders to discuss the many challenges facing the globe, as well as how the United States can continue to join with others to promote peace and prosperity and uphold sovereignty and accountability.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=97acad780185642fa6d010dd5bf648cd2eee35a24f74b24ae9530130e99387e2835fb9a17c7dbfe45e70b79757c9626da757781d3d12c3b7
Stay up to date with this week's events

President Trump Meets with Prime Minister Netanyahu of Israel

President Trump met with Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel on Monday in New York for a bilateral meeting on the sidelines of the United Nations General Assembly (UNGA), reaffirming the special relationship and unbreakable bond between the United States and Israel.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=97acad780185642f9a83aab0de9e8ce1dcf01bf0566ee1a8709fda69247151fd52aa06aa7b6345bc448e7ab3cbe6ee75b31a322e6241ea9a
Read more

President Trump Meets with President Emmanuel Macron of France

President Donald J. Trump met last night in New York with President Emmanuel Macron of France. They exchanged views on the Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action for Iran and how to curtail Iran’s destabilizing activities in the region.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=97acad780185642f615c49503b0947e15764d6a7f497af8844b73bfc34e1602fb36b9b55e59c62bad74b35e54fcdd7c0e3b2a3b9ab63f0f8
Read more

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump at the 72nd United Nations General Assembly | September 18, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)

POTUS and VP TODAY

Today, President Trump remains in New York for the second day of the 72nd Session of the United Nations General Assembly. The President will address the General Assembly today.
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=97acad780185642f206ce4a6dfc58a2c934a541430dd0ba65437d0ee5f226f9155e64b22e3b4eae598a975235761f9b59fe916e2844b7ac4
Watch the speech.

This afternoon, President Trump will participate in two bilateral meetings with the President of the United Nations and the nation of Qatar, and the President and First Lady will host a Diplomatic Reception tonight.

COMING UP

President Trump will continue participating in bilateral meetings with world leaders at the United Nations General Assembly tomorrow and Thursday.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=97acad780185642f2269ba52a06bc5161502b34aec4c38cc522ee7d5366618e46ca232e4dfd6b14f343cab7948afc161b4a1dc48e0d092f2
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=97acad780185642fd141cb9fc8abd712d864f9e6e7a66ec0b8d68be42c25d9ff0b26ae53578b5a2b3049a424c83091528971957be0cad3d4
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d49028b32075eed9ce1ff952c17ed5088f035dd0088ab6a1f1c03949ec1098a29e6ec88dce883adfa8b474999f4e11dae4
Unsubscribe

name: Tom Perez
subject: McConnell almost has enough votes to repeal Obamacare, Derek
body:
Say you'll protect affordable health care.
---
**Add your name: tell Senate Republicans you'll fight to protect affordable health care.

** **[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Stop-Health-Care-Repeal1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170919_tp_rem&source=em_20170919_tp_rem")**

Derek, the GOP won't rest until they've repealed the Affordable Care Act -- and they're poised to try to do it in the next two weeks.

Senate Republicans are about to vote on the worst repeal bill yet. If it passes, millions of Americans could lose their coverage, insurance companies would be allowed to hike up premiums, and Obamacare's protections for people with pre-existing conditions could be eliminated.

Mitch McConnell only needs 50 Republican votes to pass repeal. By some counts, he already has 48 or 49 lined up.

We need to fight back like never before. We've already proven that this grassroots movement can stop repeal. Now, we need to turn the heat all the way up.

**Step up again and sign your name. Tell the GOP you'll fight to protect our health care -- and vote them out of office if they take it away.**

**[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Stop-Health-Care-Repeal1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170919_tp_rem&source=em_20170919_tp_rem")**
Thanks -- more soon.

Tom

Tom Perez
Chair

Hello -- Here's what you need to know about the horrific Graham-Cassidy bill, the latest version of Trumpcare that Republicans are trying to jam through the Senate:
♦ An estimated 32 MILLION people would lose coverage within 10 years
♦ It would END Medicaid expansion
♦ It rolls back coverage for women's health and family planning
♦ It ends federal protections for people with pre-existing conditions, lifetime caps, and essential benefits
Reports indicate that Mitch McConnell is just one vote away from passing this devastating bill and repealing Obamacare, so each and every one of us needs to speak up now.
[**Sign our emergency petition and tell Senate Republicans NOT to pass Graham-Cassidy and to protect health care for millions of our friends, family members, and neighbors.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fde1d/2a380d31/1c44d209/fe7095/500086795/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/20170918_GrahamCassidy?source=e20170918_gc2_act&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")
You've made your voice heard before, team. That's why earlier this summer, Republicans failed to round up the votes they needed to pass their last cold-hearted bill.
But word is that John McCain, the GOP Senator often credited with stopping the health care bill from moving forward the last time around, might actually _support_ this new version.
**If we 're going to protect health care, we need to be louder than we've ever been. [Please, add your name to send a message to Senate Republicans: protect our coverage and DON'T repeal Obamacare.](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fde1d/2a380d31/1c44d209/fe7095/500086795/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/20170918_GrahamCassidy?source=e20170918_gc2_act&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")**
Thanks for rolling up your sleeves in this fight,
Team Maggie

[**We can 't let Rick Scott pull a fast one on us. Can you help us win next Tuesday's special election?**](http://act.floridadems. Begin original message from Florida Democrats:
> Friend,
>
> This month, Floridians faced down Hurricane Irma. We may still be rebuilding, but we know nothing can stop us when we join together.
>
> **Now, we need to take the same spirit to a different fight. **Even though communities are still healing after Hurricane Irma, Governor Rick Scott is refusing to reschedule the special election next week for a critical state Senate seat.
>
> Special elections have been rescheduled due to massive storms before. Rick Scott's refusal to move the special election shows he'll do _whatever_ it takes to pull out a win for the GOP. [**That 's why we need your help.**](http://act.floridadems. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/fdp-special-election-fund?amount=10&recurring=true&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&city=Englewood&state=&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=fldems&utm_content=2+-+Thatrsquos+why+we+need+your+help&utm_campaign=em091917&source=em091917&refcode=em091917")
>
> **Rick Scott is counting on Floridians ignoring this election so Republicans can win.** It is critical that we run a massive get-out-the-vote operation for next week's special election so Democrats show up and prove him wrong.
>
> [**We 're depending on grassroots gifts to make that possible. A few dollars may not seem like a lot, but together they'll power our get-out-the-vote push. Can you donate $10 or more so we can get out the vote for next week's special election?**](http://act.floridadems. Team,

According to a _New York Times_ op-ed out this week, "health care is once again in danger."

Republicans only have 11 days to pass an Obamacare repeal bill with a mere 50 votes. They are racing to get a new bill onto the floor before the September 30 deadline... and this time, they just might succeed.

John McCain -- the deciding vote against Obamacare repeal in July -- said he might be willing to support this latest bill. And with the pressure off and attention elsewhere, _the New York Times_ is right, we might see more Republican Senators flip at any moment.

[**We can 't let up now, team. Chip in $1 right now to show Republicans the consequences they'll face if they push forward with Obamacare repeal.**](http://action.dscc. Don't be fooled into thinking that after their July defeat, Republicans are working on a more responsible bill. According to _the Times_ , this new bill is just as bad, with "all the elements that made previous Republican proposals so cruel and destructive."

_The Times_ also warned that this new plan could "become law, because not enough people are taking it seriously."

**We have just 11 days left to save Obamacare and we can 't fall short. Chip in $1 right now to show Republicans we're still watching:**

[**http://www.dscc.org/11-days-to-save-Obamcare**](http://action.dscc. name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: CAP red alert: Trumpcare is back, and it's even worse
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| __ Friend,

With less than two weeks left to consider healthcare legislation under expedited Senate rules, the new 're-incarnated' Republican proposal to repeal the Affordable Care Act is now gaining traction.

_And this bill is even worse than the last one!_ It shreds Medicaid as we know it, leaves tens of millions of Americans uninsured, raises premiums, and takes a wrecking ball to our health care system.

This repeal effort represents a potential last minute game-changer for Mitch McConnell who came under harsh attack from President Trump after the failure of Trumpcare in July.

[**We need to take this threat seriously. Sign on now and stand with me against this latest Republican attack on health care for millions of Americans >>**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bd1/70cdc2ba/4698db39/397dc2b/3861969555/VEsH/ "https://cvanhollen.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/sign-help-us-defeat-republican-health-care-plans?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+We+need+to+take+this+threat+seriously+Si&utm_campaign=em091917&source=em091917")

Once again, the fate of health care in our country will come down to just THREE votes in the United States Senate.

So far only ONE Republican Senator -- Rand Paul of Kentucky -- has said he will vote against the Graham-Cassidy bill. It's still not clear if John McCain, who helped kill Trumpcare in July, will oppose this latest plan which is co-sponsored by one of his closest friends and political allies, Senator Lindsey Graham of South Carolina.

There are just 13 days left to pass a bill with just 51 votes in the U.S. Senate. After September 30 Republicans would need 60 votes to pass the same legislation. Time is running out.

[**Between now and the end of the month, it 's critical that you speak out and ask your friends in every state to call their senators to stop this latest sneak attack on our health care. We need every NO vote we can get on the Graham-Cassidy version of Trumpcare. Are you with me? **](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bd1/70cdc2ba/4698db39/397dc28/3861969555/VEsE/ "https://cvanhollen.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/sign-help-us-defeat-republican-health-care-plans?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Between+now+and+the+end+of+the+month+itr&utm_campaign=em091917&source=em091917")

Let's bury this unconscionable plan once and for all.

Chris Van Hollen
U.S. Senator

| Peter, Thousands of you responded to our survey, asking for you to identify your top 3 priorities for Congress to accomplish before Thanksgiving. Among the top priorities identified — 1) tax reform, 2) repealing the Affordable Care Act, and 3) approving funding for Hurricane Harvey relief.

Setting term limits for members of Congress is another important priority that’s quickly gaining traction among conservative voters as midterm elections loom around the corner. What we’ve found through these surveys is that what matters most to Christians, perhaps even more than any specific legislative item, is that Congress be held accountable. It’s clear, you want measures in place, chief of which are term limits, so the representatives and senators who are obstructing the due process of lawmaking can be held accountable. | | [See the Full Survey Results ►](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Fcongress-priorities-survey-results%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=170919_sr_s6&n=3)

name: Russ Feingold, LegitAction
subject: One incredibly simple way to improve turnout
body:
[ Legit ]LegitAction

Friend,

Our system of government -- the balance of power, the accountability
to the people -- only works if people vote. It is that simple.

And there is one incredibly simple way to get more people to vote that
we as a nation must start taking advantage of: AVR, or automatic voter
registration.

AVR involves two steps:

1. Any time an eligible voter interacts with government agencies, he
or she is registered to vote or has his/her voting information
updated.
2. Those agencies electronically relay the information to appropriate
state electoral officials.

The results are more registered voters, more up-to-date voter rolls,
and more people voting on election day.

AVR is now standard practice in 10 states and the District of
Columbia, and legislation has been introduced in as many as 30 other
states -- as well as in Congress.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/sign/congress-avr-automatic-voter-registration-petition/?t=1002&akid=275%2E356062%2EbWHskX ]Sign LegitAction’s petition now and urge members of Congress to
support the Automatic Voter Registration bills sponsored in the House
by Rep. Brady and in the Senate by Sen. Leahy.

A sham election commission, a national voter database, and an invasion
of citizens’ privacy don’t fix any problems with our voting system --
unless your problem is too many of the ‘wrong kind of people’ voting.

The benefits to our democratic legitimacy should be a case-closed
argument for AVR, but there are other reasons AVR makes total sense:
it is cheaper and less error-prone than the current paper process, and
it would clean up existing voter rolls and reduce the potential for
phony voter roll purges and other shady efforts at disenfranchisement.

And yes, it would reduce the potential for voter fraud, though voter
fraud is virtually non-existent in this country.

If members of Congress are serious about wanting to improve our
elections, let’s see them champion AVR and support the Brady and Leahy
measures right away.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/sign/congress-avr-automatic-voter-registration-petition/?t=1003&akid=275%2E356062%2EbWHskX ]Tell members of Congress to support the Brady and Leahy AVR
measures. Sign our petition today.

Rather than raising concerns about purging voter rolls and voter
suppression, the country has an opportunity to champion voting rights
and actually improve the legitimacy of our elections and our
democracy.

Let’s take it.

Thank you for standing up for our democratic legitimacy today,

Russ Feingold

clean_body: [ Legit ]LegitAction Friend, Our system of government -- the balance of power, the accountability to the people -- only works if people vote. It is that simple. And there is one incredibly simple way to get more people to vote that we as a nation must start taking advantage of: AVR, or automatic voter registration. AVR involves two steps: 1. Any time an eligible voter interacts with government agencies, he or she is registered to vote or has his/her voting information updated. 2. Those agencies electronically relay the information to appropriate state electoral officials. The results are more registered voters, more up-to-date voter rolls, and more people voting on election day. AVR is now standard practice in 10 states and the District of Columbia, and legislation has been introduced in as many as 30 other states -- as well as in Congress. [ ]Sign LegitAction’s petition now and urge members of Congress to support the Automatic Voter Registration bills sponsored in the House by Rep. Brady and in the Senate by Sen. Leahy. A sham election commission, a national voter database, and an invasion of citizens’ privacy don’t fix any problems with our voting system -- unless your problem is too many of the ‘wrong kind of people’ voting. The benefits to our democratic legitimacy should be a case-closed argument for AVR, but there are other reasons AVR makes total sense: it is cheaper and less error-prone than the current paper process, and it would clean up existing voter rolls and reduce the potential for phony voter roll purges and other shady efforts at disenfranchisement. And yes, it would reduce the potential for voter fraud, though voter fraud is virtually non-existent in this country. If members of Congress are serious about wanting to improve our elections, let’s see them champion AVR and support the Brady and Leahy measures right away. [ ]Tell members of Congress to support the Brady and Leahy AVR measures. Sign our petition today. Rather than raising concerns about purging voter rolls and voter suppression, the country has an opportunity to champion voting rights and actually improve the legitimacy of our elections and our democracy. Let’s take it. Thank you for standing up for our democratic legitimacy today, Russ Feingold [ ]TAKE ACTION » Authorized and paid for by LegitAction, Inc. disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail
body:
| |

| |

| Friend, The thing about climate change is that it waits for nobody. It doesn't care if you're on vacation or out to brunch. It doesn't care if your schedule is booked or you don't believe in it. **Climate change is real, getting worse with time, and is one of the most pressing issues facing humanity.** EPA Administrator Scott Pruitt doesn't think it's time to talk about climate change. Instead, he wants to continue censoring scientists and shutting down attempts to combat it. [_**We can 't afford to wait any longer -- the EPA and the Trump Administration need to face reality. Tell Scott Pruitt: the time to talk about climate change is NOW.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113e3c/622b7b5b/5f71896/307bd589/3191183868/VEsH/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e170919") Unless immediate action is taken to combat climate change, hurricanes will be more intense, floods will worsen, fires will affect more acres, droughts will become more severe, and catastrophic weather events will increase in frequency. **Scott Pruitt may believe it is "insensitive" to discuss climate change, but I think it is insensitive to refuse to recognize the reality that our planet is facing.** We have the opportunity to mitigate some of the worst consequences of climate change. But we can only do that if we recognize the threat that climate change represents and take action. [_**We 're done waiting. It's time to act. Add your voice to mine and call on the EPA and the Trump Administration to address climate change.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113e3c/622b7b5b/5f71896/307bd589/3191183868/VEsE/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e170919") Thank you, Debbie |

Friend, I have been humbled by the incredible outpouring of support I've received since announcing my run for U.S. Senate. The people who have come out for us already send a clear message that we need a proven conservative with a consistent track record to take on Tammy Baldwin, and I’m hoping you’ll join us today. We’ve got pro-life advocates, business leaders, conservative champions, grassroots organizers and elected officials. Just check out these leaders, like Ave Bie, Lt. Gov. Margaret Farrow, Sen. Scott Fitzgerald, Michael Grebe, Mary Kohler, Sue Lynch, Barbara Lyons, Sandra Mills, Gerard Randall, Donald Taylor, Jim Troupis, Jackie Trudell, and Jerry Whitburn.They’re all on Team Leah, and I’m hoping you will be too: Moving forward, my team and I are working to continue growing our network across the state so we can take Wisconsin’s bold conservative reforms to Washington. And friend, I want to make sure you're a part of it! If you haven't already, will you join Team Leah today: Friend, I know that if we work together, we CAN bring conservative change to Washington. I hope to see you on board! Thanks! Leah Paid for by Leah for Senate
